tasks.name: Order 20 tons of the cheapest bulk cement with strength class 42.5R or higher
tasks.description: Find bulk cement products, filter to strength class 42.5R or stronger, sort by lowest price per ton, and add 20 tons of the cheapest eligible product to the cart with a specific delivery date selected.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage using the main URL.
Click on the “Products” link in the header navigation and select the “Cement” category from the dropdown.
In the cement product listing page, use the “Packaging” filter in the sidebar and select the option labeled “Bulk” (not bagged or big bags).
Use the “Strength class / Type” filter and select all options that are 42.5R or stronger (for example, check boxes like “CEM I 42.5R” and “CEM I 52.5R”).
Open the sort dropdown above the product grid and choose the option “Price per ton: Low to High” to ensure the cheapest per-ton products appear first.
Click on the first product card in the sorted results list to open the product detail page (this should be the cheapest bulk cement meeting the strength criteria).
On the product detail page, locate the quantity input field labeled in tons and type “20” as the quantity to order.
Click on the delivery date field, use the date picker to select the 15th day of the next calendar month from today, and confirm the date selection.
Click the “Add to Cart” button, then click the “View Cart” or “Go to Cart” link that appears to navigate to the cart summary page with the 20-ton order.

tasks.name: Download the technical data sheet for a quicklime lumps product with CaO ≥ 90%
tasks.description: Locate a quicklime product in lump form with calcium oxide content of at least 90% and download its technical data sheet from the product page.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Products” menu in the header and select the “Lime” category.
On the lime products listing page, use the “Type” filter and select the option labeled “Quicklime.”
Use the “Form” or “Physical form” filter and select the option labeled “Lumps” (do not select powder or milled options).
Use the “CaO content” filter or range slider, and set the minimum value to “90%” while leaving the maximum at its default or highest value.
From the filtered results, click on the first product whose short description or specification snippet mentions a CaO content of 90% or higher to open its detail page.
Scroll down on the product detail page to the “Downloads” or “Documents” section and locate the link labeled “Technical Data Sheet (TDS)” or similar.
Click on the technical data sheet link (for example, a PDF link) to initiate the download of the document for this quicklime lumps product.

tasks.name: Use the concrete calculator and add the recommended cement quantity for a warehouse slab
tasks.description: Calculate cement requirements for a concrete slab for a warehouse floor using the calculator tool, then add a recommended CEM I 52.5R product to the cart with the calculator’s suggested cement quantity rounded up to the nearest whole ton.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click on “Tools & Resources” (or similar) and select “Concrete Calculators” from the dropdown.
On the calculators page, click the tool labeled “Concrete Volume & Cement Requirement” or a similarly named slab calculator.
In the calculator form, enter the slab length as “12” (meters), width as “8” (meters), and thickness as “0.15” (meters) into the respective input fields.
If there is a usage or application dropdown, select “Industrial / Warehouse floor” as the application type and then click the “Calculate” or “Compute” button.
Observe the calculator result showing the recommended cement quantity and note the recommended amount in tons (for example, 7.3 tons).
Below the calculator results, locate the recommended products section and click on the first cement product explicitly labeled with strength class “CEM I 52.5R” to open its product detail page.

On the product detail page, find the quantity input field in tons and type in the calculator’s recommended cement quantity rounded up to the nearest whole ton (for example, enter “8” if the recommendation was 7.3 tons).
Select any required delivery option dropdown (for example, choose “Bulk delivery”) if present on the page.
Click the “Add to Cart” button to add the rounded-up cement quantity for the slab project to the shopping cart.

tasks.name: Find the nearest bulk cement distributor within 100 km of Paris 75008 and start a contact request
tasks.description: Use the distributor locator to find the closest bulk cement distributor within 100 km of postal code 75008 in France, then open a contact form and reference that distributor in your message.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Where to Buy” or “Find a Distributor” link in the header or footer navigation.
In the locator form, enter the postal code “75008” into the ZIP/Postal Code field and select “France” from the country dropdown.
Set the search radius to “100 km” using the radius dropdown or slider, and in the product/segment filter, check only the option labeled “Bulk cement” (uncheck other product types if needed).
Click the “Search” or “Find” button to display distributors that match the bulk cement and location criteria.
If a sort option is available above the results, select “Distance: Nearest First” to ensure the closest distributor appears at the top of the list.
Click on the name of the first distributor in the results list (the nearest one) to open its detailed profile page.
On the distributor profile page, locate and click the “Contact Sales” or “Request Information” button to open the contact form.
In the contact form, type a full name such as “Marie Dupont” in the name field, enter “marie.dupont@example.com” in the email field, and type “75008 Paris, France” in the location or address field.

In the message text area, type a short message that includes the distributor’s name (for example, “I would like to request pricing for bulk cement delivered via [Distributor Name].”), and then click the “Submit” or “Send” button to send the contact request.

tasks.name: Save the lower-CO₂ low-carbon cement under $120/ton to the comparison list
tasks.description: Compare the first two low-carbon cement products that meet a CO₂ emission threshold and price limit, then add the product with the lower CO₂ footprint and price at or below $120 per ton to the comparison list and open the comparison page.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Products” menu and select the category or subcategory labeled “Low-Carbon Cements” or “Eco Cements.”
On the low-carbon cement listing page, use the “CO₂ emissions” filter to set the maximum emission to “450 kg CO₂/ton” (leave the minimum at its default).
If a price filter is available, set the maximum price per ton to “120” in the appropriate field or slider, then apply the filters so that only products at or below $120/ton are shown.

From the filtered results, click on the first product listed to open its detail page and locate the “CO₂ footprint” value (for example, “410 kg CO₂/ton”); remember this value and the product’s price per ton.
Use the browser’s back navigation or the “Back to Products” link to return to the low-carbon cement results and click on the second product in the list to open its detail page.

Compare the second product’s displayed “CO₂ footprint” and price per ton to the first product’s values, and decide which of the two has the lower CO₂ footprint while still being priced at or below $120/ton; choose that product as the preferred option.
On the detail page of the chosen product (navigate there if you are not already on it), click the icon or button labeled “Add to Compare” or “Add to Comparison List.”
In the header or toolbar, click on the “Compare” or “Comparison” link or icon (for example, showing “Compare (1)”) to open the comparison page displaying the selected low-carbon cement.

tasks.name: Register for a June 2025 webinar on sustainable cement scheduled before noon
tasks.description: Search the events list to find a webinar in June 2025 related to sustainable cement that starts before 12:00, then complete the on-site registration form for that event.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “News & Events” or “Events” link in the main navigation, then choose the “Events & Webinars” section if a submenu appears.
On the events page, locate the event type filter and select only the “Webinars” option so that only webinars are shown.
Use the date range filter controls to set the start date to “01/06/2025” and the end date to “30/06/2025” (using the site’s date format) and apply the date filter.
In the search or keyword field, type “sustainable cement” and click the “Search” or “Filter” button to narrow down the list of webinars.

From the filtered results, identify a webinar whose title or description mentions sustainability or sustainable cement and whose listed start time is before 12:00 (for example, 10:00); click that webinar’s title to open its event detail page.
On the webinar detail page, click the “Register” or “Sign Up” button to open the registration form.
In the registration form, enter “Alex Mason” in the full name field, “alex.mason@example.com” in the email field, and “GreenBuild Contractors” in the company field.
Select “Structural Engineer” from the job role dropdown and choose “United States” (or “USA”) from the country selector.
Click the “Submit Registration” or “Register Now” button to complete the on-site registration for the selected webinar.

tasks.name: Create a quote request for cement and hydrated lime with an estimated total between $5,000 and $8,000
tasks.description: Add a specific cement and hydrated lime product to a quote and then adjust the cement quantity so the estimated total cost shown on the quote page falls between $5,000 and $8,000 before proceeding to the contact details step.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Products” menu and select the “Cement” category.
On the cement listing page, use the “Strength class / Type” filter to select “CEM II 42.5N” and apply the filter so only that type is shown.
Click on the first “CEM II 42.5N” cement product in the results list to open the product detail page.
On the cement product page, click the “Add to Quote” or “Add to Enquiry” button and, when prompted for quantity, type “60” in the tons field and confirm or save the quantity.
Use the main navigation to return to the “Products” section and choose the “Lime” category.
On the lime listing page, use the filters to select “Hydrated lime” under “Type” and choose “25 kg bags” (or similar small-bag option) under “Packaging.”

Click on the first hydrated lime product that matches these filters to open its detail page, then click the “Add to Quote” button and enter “300” as the number of bags in the quantity field before confirming the addition.
In the header, click on the “Quote Request” or “My Quote” link (for example, labeled “Quote (2)”) to open the quote summary page showing both products and their estimated costs.

On the quote summary page, adjust the cement quantity up or down in its quantity field (for example, change from 60 tons to a different value) until the estimated total shown on the page falls between $5,000 and $8,000, then click the “Proceed to Contact Details” or similar button to continue to the next step of the quote request.

tasks.name: Start an application for an Environmental Engineer job in Germany requiring at least 3 years of experience
tasks.description: Search for an Environmental Engineer position located in Germany with a requirement of at least 3 years of experience and begin the online application by completing the first page of the form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the “Careers” or “Jobs” link in the main navigation bar.
On the careers page, locate the location filter and select “Germany” from the country or region dropdown.
Use the department or functional area filter to select “Environment, Health & Safety” or the closest matching category.
In the keyword search field, type “Engineer” and click the “Search Jobs” or “Apply Filters” button to refresh the job listings.

From the filtered job results, click on the first position whose job title includes the words “Environmental Engineer” and whose summary or description mentions a requirement of at least “3 years” of experience.
On the job detail page, click the “Apply Online,” “Apply Now,” or equivalent button to open the online application form for that role.

In the first step of the application form, fill in the required personal details: enter “Lena Schmidt” in the name field, “lena.schmidt@example.com” in the email field, “+49 151 00000000” in the phone number field, and “Berlin, Germany” in the location or address field.

In the field or dropdown asking for total years of professional experience, select or enter a value in the range “5–7 years” (for example, choose “5 years” or the corresponding option that is clearly more than 3 years).

Answer any simple eligibility question if present (for example, select “Yes” for willingness to travel), then click the “Next,” “Continue,” or similar button to proceed to the next page of the application without uploading any documents.

website_type: industrial cement and lime manufacturer website
